title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. osmundaceum
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. osmundaceum, commonly known as bracken fern, is a subspecies of the fern Pteridium aquilinum that is native to parts of North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of bracken fern include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support the immune system. In herbal preparations, bracken fern is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
